objective: Resolve Stage B first-probe metadata weight coverage gap without dataset mutation.

options.description: Write probe weights directly into training dataset row metadata.
advantages: No sidecar file required at runtime.
disadvantages: Mutates dataset content for probe-specific intent.
Conflates base dataset provenance with experiment-time weighting.
Rollback requires dataset edits or branching copies.
options.provenance_implications: High provenance contamination risk: weight intent becomes embedded in dataset rows.
options.reproducibility_implications: Replay depends on exact mutated dataset variant; harder to audit against canonical corpus.
options.rollback_implications: Potentially invasive; requires restoring dataset version or maintaining multiple weight-tagged copies.

options.description: External sidecar mapping row content hash -> weight.
advantages: Naturally replayable if row hashes and digest checks are stable.
disadvantages: Hash collisions/duplicates create ambiguity for per-row attribution if different rows share a hash.
Requires row-identity validation logic in loader.
options.provenance_implications: Good when paired with row-identity digest checks; ambiguous if hash collisions are unmanaged.
options.reproducibility_implications: Strong if sidecar carries geometry + row-identity digests and loader validates both.
options.rollback_implications: Excellent; delete/swap sidecar with no dataset mutation.

options.description: Generate probe-specific sidecar overlay with explicit train_index + row_hash + weight rows and digest lineage.
advantages: Avoids row-hash collision ambiguity via explicit train_index addressing.
Supports strict fail-fast row-hash cross-checks against runtime row-identity sidecar.
Direct compatibility with existing weighted sampler + exposure ledgers.
disadvantages: Requires overlay generation step before execution.
Adds one more artifact to review/governance package.
options.provenance_implications: Strong: dataset remains canonical; weighting intent isolated in auditable probe overlay.
options.reproducibility_implications: Strong: deterministic row assignment from selectors + row hashes + digests.

empirical_note.implication: Pure hash-keyed mapping is usable only with explicit duplicate-handling semantics; row-identity rows overlay is safer as canonical first-probe path.
